source_anchor_or_hint: Required source ladder and no source law
source_summary: The task states that local Builder sources are required for this self-build campaign and that external domains require trusted sources before content is authored.
lesson: The school generator may expand knowledge only from explicit local evidence; a campaign seed without source_path, source_summary, and proof anchor is not valid memory material.
negative_trap: Do not manufacture domain knowledge or generic Builder lessons just because a root needs more rows.
proof_target: Candidate contains campaign_id, seed_id, source_path, source_summary, and a local source path that exists in the repo.
behavior_delta: When planning a campaign, Builder checks the source ladder before generation and rejects unsourced seeds instead of filling gaps with template variation.
return_to_parent: Report the source-backed seed identity and keep unsupported external topics blocked until Owner supplies sources.

source_summary: The level plan marks selected campaign roots and uses higher continuation levels from the compact memory audit instead of blindly using cursor next_level=1.
lesson: Cursor next_level=1 is not sufficient authority when compact memory already proves higher observed levels; campaign generation must continue from the reconciled level plan.
negative_trap: Do not reset every root to level 1 just because theme_cursor_ledger was rebuilt as a seed ledger.
proof_target: Generated campaign candidates for this root use start_level from the level plan rather than a blind level 1 reset.
behavior_delta: Builder treats cursor state, memory snapshot, and journal evidence as separate signals and chooses the reconciled continuation level.
return_to_parent: Return the chosen start level and source report path with any generated lesson that depends on cursor continuation.

source_summary: The streaming lane validates each candidate batch, produces ready atoms, quarantines duplicate or placeholder stream keys, and does not mutate active memory.
lesson: Streaming absorption is a batch-ready lane, not memory promotion; a seed-backed candidate must survive contract validation and stream-key uniqueness before it can become a ready atom.
negative_trap: Do not claim memory learning merely because streaming produced ready_atoms.
proof_target: Streaming report shows ready_atoms_total equals generated candidates, stream_quarantined_total=0, and active_memory_mutated=false.
behavior_delta: Builder separates streaming readiness from digest or accepted memory claims and reports the exact lane status.
return_to_parent: Return ready/quarantine counts and keep promotion claims out of parent summaries unless later gates prove them.

source_summary: The consistency validator compares aggregate contract validation with per-batch validation for the same generated material.
lesson: A candidate run is not validated by a single aggregate pass unless the per-batch totals match processed, accepted, and rejected counts.
negative_trap: Do not hide batch-level rejects behind a green aggregate report.
proof_target: Consistency output reports matching aggregate and per-batch accepted/rejected totals with status PASS_CODEX_CURRICULUM_CONTRACT_CONSISTENCY_V1.
behavior_delta: Builder reports validation as a multi-surface consistency result and stops on mismatch instead of smoothing it over.
return_to_parent: Return aggregate and per-batch counts together when summarizing campaign validation.

source_summary: The coverage audit records memory, cursor, and journal signals with priority and recommended seed counts for each root.
lesson: Campaign focus should come from observed weak or high-priority roots, not from alphabetical iteration or count pressure.
negative_trap: Do not spend a serious run on roots just because they appear first in a topic list.
proof_target: Campaign pack roots are selected from the level plan and each seed references the audit or another local source.
behavior_delta: Builder chooses campaign material by weakness and evidence, then records why each root was included.
return_to_parent: Return the selected root, priority reason, and source report path instead of only returning the generated count.

source_summary: The canonical school contract says the owner-facing launch is run_agent_school.ps1 with Count, Mode, and TopicsPlan only.
lesson: There is one active school entrypoint; Count changes are launch parameters and must not create another school or generator surface.
negative_trap: Do not fork a new school or edit code just because Owner asks for 15k, 50k, or 1M.
proof_target: Launch command uses operations/school/run_agent_school.ps1 -Count N -Mode Test|Live -TopicsPlan path.
behavior_delta: Builder routes size changes to launch parameters and reserves code edits for actual generator or validator behavior changes.
return_to_parent: Return the canonical command shape and confirm no duplicate organ was created.

source_anchor_or_hint: topics roots, weights, verbs, and modes
source_summary: The topics plan declares Builder curriculum roots, weights, verbs, and source modes used by the school route.
lesson: Curriculum control belongs in the topics plan plus campaign pack, not in hardcoded one-theme generator loops.
negative_trap: Do not treat a hardcoded topic stream as a serious campaign just because it can produce many rows.
proof_target: Generated candidates include topic_plan and campaign_pack fields, and roots come from the selected campaign plan.
behavior_delta: Builder checks curriculum inputs before generation and can explain which plan or pack controlled the run.
return_to_parent: Return the topic plan path and campaign pack path used for the candidate batch.

source_anchor_or_hint: source selection then internal factory call
source_summary: The source router selects a bounded material supplier and then invokes the internal factory without allowing sources to mutate compact memory.
lesson: Source routing chooses material supply authority only; it does not decide route truth, write memory, or bypass validators.
negative_trap: Do not let a source port or campaign pack become an unvalidated direct memory writer.
proof_target: Source router proof records selected_source and factory_status while active memory remains untouched.
behavior_delta: Builder treats source selection, candidate generation, and memory promotion as separate gates.
return_to_parent: Return selected source, factory status, and the boundary that the source cannot write compact memory.

source_anchor_or_hint: factory memory surfaces and ladder contract
source_summary: Factory memory tracks roots, verbs, levels, source modes, learning_key, and prerequisite_key without promoting active memory.
lesson: Factory memory is a generation ledger and ladder guide; it is not proof of live learning and does not promote accepted memory by itself.
negative_trap: Do not call a generated learning_key accepted memory before validators and digest gates run.
proof_target: Candidate includes learning_key, prerequisite_key when level>1, cursor_reserved_level, and active_memory_mutated=false in reports.
behavior_delta: Builder uses factory memory to avoid duplicates while keeping acceptance claims for later proof surfaces.
return_to_parent: Return the factory memory boundary and the generated key that was reserved.

source_summary: The streaming validator passes only when streaming sees batches, ready atoms, and no active memory mutation.
lesson: Ready lane status means candidates are prepared for later gates; it is weaker than digest, recall/use, or accepted memory proof.
negative_trap: Do not equate ready_atoms with behavior delta or memory retention.
proof_target: Validator output includes PASS_STREAMING_SCHOOL_TO_ABSORPTION_VALIDATION_V1, READY_ATOMS, STREAM_QUARANTINED, and ACTIVE_MEMORY_MUTATED=false.
behavior_delta: Builder reports the ready lane as a bounded pre-memory state and waits for later proof before claiming learning.
return_to_parent: Return ready lane counts and say what proof remains before memory claims are allowed.

source_anchor_or_hint: canonical flow includes digest pipeline for Live
source_summary: The canonical flow sends Live runs through streaming, digest pipeline, compact memory recall/use proof, and canonical proof.
lesson: Digest belongs to Live school flow after streaming; Test mode should validate ready-lane curriculum without mutating compact memory.
negative_trap: Do not run digest or mutate active memory inside a Codex slice that is bounded to candidate tests.
proof_target: Mode=Test outputs show active_memory_mutated=false; Live digest is left for Owner after acceptance.
behavior_delta: Builder keeps Test validation and Live digestion distinct and does not sneak memory writes into code-validation work.
return_to_parent: Return whether the current run was Test or Live and what digest work was intentionally deferred.

source_anchor_or_hint: Live requires compact memory recall/use proof
source_summary: The canonical Live path requires recall/use proof and behavior_delta before full proof status can be claimed.
lesson: Memory is not useful until a later probe shows it can be recalled and changes a decision; candidate count alone is never behavior delta.
negative_trap: Do not say the Builder learned because 15k candidates were generated or streamed.
proof_target: Live proof must include recall_use_status and behavior_delta=true after digest, not just factory_candidates or ready_atoms.
behavior_delta: Builder asks for recall/use evidence before upgrading a campaign from generated material to learned behavior.
return_to_parent: Return the missing recall/use proof requirement when only Test validation has run.

source_summary: AGENTS.md forbids claiming fixed, works, accepted, synced, clean, complete, or runtime_ready=true without fresh proof.
lesson: Proof language must match evidence: CODEX_DRAFT for edits, VALIDATOR_PASS for bounded validators, and runtime_ready=false unless Owner-authorized proof says otherwise.
negative_trap: Do not upgrade status words because a patch looks reasonable or a previous report existed.
proof_target: Final report uses bounded statuses, lists validators, and keeps RUNTIME_READY=false.
behavior_delta: Builder uses precise proof vocabulary and avoids confidence inflation in school, memory, and runtime claims.
return_to_parent: Return the strongest status actually supported by this run and name the missing proof for any stronger claim.

source_anchor_or_hint: Bounded tests only and forbidden Live long run
source_summary: The task allows candidate factory Test runs and streaming on 100, while forbidding Count=50000, Count=1000000, and long Live runs.
lesson: Codex may patch and run bounded Test validation, but first Live validation at 15k belongs to Owner after accepting the draft.
negative_trap: Do not launch 15k Live from Codex just because the pack has expansion_budget=15000.
proof_target: Command history contains only TargetAccepted=25, TargetAccepted=100, and optional streaming validation; no Mode=Live run.
behavior_delta: Builder stops at bounded validation and returns the exact Owner launch boundary for Live.
return_to_parent: Return the next recommended Live size without launching it.

source_summary: AGENTS.md says .runtime is ignored evidence/temp material, must not be committed, and cleanup needs process checks and compact proof first.
lesson: Runtime output is evidence for validation, not tracked campaign content; Codex must not delete runtime surfaces or assume active memory exists after cleanup.
negative_trap: Do not inspect or mutate .runtime active memory as if it were present after Owner intentionally removed it.
proof_target: Validation reports active_memory_mutated=false and Git changes exclude .runtime content.
behavior_delta: Builder treats runtime paths as transient and reports only compact paths or commands needed for Owner inspection.
return_to_parent: Return runtime report paths only as evidence, never as committed source or accepted memory.

source_summary: AGENTS.md states Codex is a bounded builder, repair, audit, and launcher tool, not Builder brain or accepted truth source.
lesson: Codex-authored campaign content is CODEX_DRAFT until validators and Owner acceptance; it cannot become the Builder brain by writing a pack.
negative_trap: Do not let campaign seeds override contracts, route locks, active memory, or Owner decisions.
proof_target: Final report says Existing organ updated=YES, New organ created=NO, and COMMIT=NONE unless explicitly authorized.
behavior_delta: Builder keeps source authority, code authority, validation authority, and Owner acceptance separate.
return_to_parent: Return exactly what Codex drafted and what still needs acceptance.

source_summary: AGENTS.md requires task understanding, in/out of scope, expected reads/changes, plans, stop rules, and a PREFLIGHT_DECISION before file edits.
lesson: No file writes are allowed before bounded preflight passes; preflight must be file-aware and validation-aware.
negative_trap: Do not patch first and backfill a plan after the fact.
proof_target: Conversation includes PREFLIGHT_PASS and Files changed before PREFLIGHT_PASS: NO before apply_patch edits.
behavior_delta: Builder pauses for bounded preflight even during urgent repair work and reports any blocker batch before mutation.
return_to_parent: Return the preflight decision and any pre-existing unrelated dirty state separately from task edits.

source_anchor_or_hint: Do NOT replace school with a new mechanism
source_summary: The task clarifies that the school remains the learning accelerator and Codex authors content before a serious campaign.
lesson: A campaign pack is source material for the existing school, not a parallel direct-answer or external-brain organ.
negative_trap: Do not create a new direct-answer organ when the generator lacks deep content.
proof_target: Only files under existing candidate_factory/campaign_packs and existing generator/validator are changed.
behavior_delta: Builder improves the current school route instead of bypassing it when generator quality is weak.
return_to_parent: Return the existing organ path that was updated and explicitly state New organ created=NO.

source_summary: The task defines exact deliverables, bounded tests, expected reports, and acceptance boundaries for the campaign generator update.
lesson: A bounded Codex slice must answer the current slice only and must not redo committed earlier work or silently expand scope.
negative_trap: Do not rerun slice 1 coverage authoring or start a broad repo audit when slice 2 says use committed outputs.
proof_target: Final report references existing coverage audit and level plan rather than regenerating them.
behavior_delta: Builder reads the current task package, identifies reuse inputs, and limits edits to the requested slice.
return_to_parent: Return the slice boundary and the reused slice 1 artifact paths.

source_summary: The task forbids report/runtime bloat and asks campaign packs to store seeds/templates rather than expanded million rows.
lesson: Reports should summarize validation and grounding without committing expanded candidates or large runtime traces.
negative_trap: Do not write 15k, 50k, or 1M expanded candidate rows into Git.
proof_target: Campaign pack is compact JSONL with expansion_budget and validation reports are bounded to 25 and 100 tests.
behavior_delta: Builder writes source seeds and compact proofs, leaving mass expansion to runtime parameters.
return_to_parent: Return report size impact and note that expanded runtime data is not tracked content.

source_summary: AGENTS.md warns against reading or creating broad report/runtime bloat and prefers exact files and active validators/proofs only.
lesson: Validation artifacts should be small, named, and directly tied to the bounded run, not a sprawling archive of generated state.
negative_trap: Do not create new report folders for every candidate or dump large JSONL traces into tracked reports.
proof_target: Only compact validator summaries are written under existing report surfaces; .runtime remains untracked.
behavior_delta: Builder chooses one focused validator report and avoids multiplying historical maps or logs.
return_to_parent: Return the compact report path and omit raw candidate dumps from the tracked delivery summary.

source_summary: The journal records that .runtime and heavy reports were intentionally removed, while a compact evidence snapshot was preserved for generator design.
lesson: After cleanup, Codex must not assume active .runtime memory exists; it should use the preserved snapshot and avoid deleting runtime evidence itself.
negative_trap: Do not restore deleted runtime folders or treat missing active memory as a validation failure for this slice.
proof_target: Generator and campaign validator work from preserved snapshot/source files and do not require .runtime/active_compact_semantic_memory_v1.
behavior_delta: Builder respects cleanup boundaries and routes active memory proof to later Owner-terminal Live validation.
return_to_parent: Return that active compact memory was not mutated and that preserved evidence snapshot was used only as source evidence.

source_anchor_or_hint: Repo identity, branch, and commit policy
source_summary: AGENTS.md requires H:\efab, branch main, origin efab.git, and no commits unless explicitly allowed.
lesson: Repo identity and dirty state are preflight gates; Codex may leave intended diffs uncommitted when commit is not authorized.
negative_trap: Do not commit campaign files or hide unrelated dirty files without explicit Owner permission.
proof_target: Preflight reports branch main, origin URL, HEAD sync, and final COMMIT=NONE if no commit was requested.
behavior_delta: Builder treats Git state as part of safety, not as a cosmetic final step.
return_to_parent: Return changed files and unrelated pre-existing untracked state separately.

source_anchor_or_hint: Remote identity gate and commit policy
source_summary: The active remote must resolve to the expected GitHub efab repository and Codex must not push or commit unless asked.
lesson: GitHub sync is identity evidence, not permission to publish; a local draft remains uncommitted until Owner requests commit/push.
negative_trap: Do not open PRs or push campaign changes from this slice without explicit authorization.
proof_target: Final report includes origin URL/branch evidence and commit status without remote mutation.
behavior_delta: Builder verifies sync state and keeps publication as a separate Owner decision.
return_to_parent: Return whether HEAD was even with origin/main at preflight and what remains uncommitted.

source_anchor_or_hint: Mode Test maps to RunKind Test
source_summary: The canonical entrypoint maps Mode=Test to RunKind=Test and Mode=Live to RunKind=Real before invoking factory and downstream gates.
lesson: Local Test mode can validate candidate generation and streaming without live memory mutation; Live mode is a separate Owner-selected run.
negative_trap: Do not blur Test and Live just because both use the same Count parameter.
proof_target: Validation commands use RunKind Test or Mode Test and report runtime_ready=false.
behavior_delta: Builder chooses local Test for Codex validation and refuses to start Live from this slice.
return_to_parent: Return the exact mode used by each validator command.

source_summary: The journal records Owner correction that the existing organ should be updated and the school should not be bypassed by a replacement mechanism.
lesson: A helper inside candidate_factory is allowed only when it is part of the existing organ; new runtime or sandbox organs are out of scope.
negative_trap: Do not create a parallel generator directory, sandbox runtime, or second school route to make validation easier.
proof_target: Changed paths remain under operations/school/curriculum/candidate_factory except compact existing report outputs.
behavior_delta: Builder repairs the current body in place and reports New organ created=NO.
return_to_parent: Return the updated existing organ path and explicitly list no new organ directory.

source_anchor_or_hint: Proof execution budget and no validation loop
source_summary: AGENTS.md allows static checks, syntax checks, and one scoped quick smoke check, but forbids repair/validate/retry loops and long proof supervision.
lesson: Codex validation should be bounded and single-pass; after a failed proof, report exact failure and stop unless validation loops are explicitly allowed.
negative_trap: Do not keep patching and rerunning until a long proof turns green.
proof_target: Only the planned bounded 25/100/streaming validations are run and no retry loop is hidden.
behavior_delta: Builder treats failed bounded validation as evidence to report, not as permission for an unbounded repair cycle.
return_to_parent: Return the exact command output and stop condition if validation fails.

source_anchor_or_hint: Final report standard and validation plan
source_summary: AGENTS.md requires validation evidence, files changed, diff summary, risks, limitations, commit status, and runtime_ready=false in the final report.
lesson: A patch is not delivered until its bounded validation result and changed-file surface are reported.
negative_trap: Do not stop after writing code without running the agreed quick validators.
proof_target: Final report lists validator commands and exact stdout key-values for 25, 100, and streaming 100 if run.
behavior_delta: Builder closes the loop with evidence instead of relying on intention or code review alone.
return_to_parent: Return the validator outputs and the strongest supported readiness status.

source_anchor_or_hint: Owner correction: campaign content, not replacement mechanism
source_summary: The journal records Owner correction that Codex should author deep campaign content for the existing school rather than bypassing the school.
lesson: Owner corrections update the route immediately; old shallow-run momentum must stop rather than continue under a stale interpretation.
negative_trap: Do not defend the previous 50k/1M shallow route after Owner points out it produced template variation.
proof_target: Campaign pack contains source-grounded lessons and generator output reports campaign_seeded_candidates rather than generic fallback rows.
behavior_delta: Builder incorporates corrections into the next code path and explicitly reports how the route changed.
return_to_parent: Return the correction honored and the concrete file change that implements it.

source_summary: The factory doc says Codex designs or repairs the factory, local factory produces candidates, school validates, and promotion remains separate.
lesson: Every generated lesson should carry a return-to-parent instruction that compresses what was learned and names the remaining gate before behavior changes.
negative_trap: Do not let child candidate generation become an unbounded task with no parent summary or proof boundary.
proof_target: Each seed-backed candidate includes return_to_parent and behavior_use_proof_target fields that survive contract validation.
behavior_delta: Builder summarizes candidate outcomes back to the parent task with evidence and next gate, instead of drifting into generation volume.
return_to_parent: Compress accepted lessons only after validation and report source, proof target, and behavior boundary to the parent task.
